co_department_bot_supervisors.advancement_condition: Must reach 100% in current phase. Phase promotion granted only by starrm1.

nutrition_support_routes: Enteral (NG, NJ, G, J, PEG, PEJ)

co_department_bot_supervisors.current_focus_areas: Independent study of nutrition support routes: Parenteral (Peripheral/Central), Enteral (NG, NJ, G, J, PEG, PEJ), and Oral routes. Enteral formula types (Elemental, Peptide-Based, High Protein, Standard Polymeric). Disease-specific nutrition including TPN components and monitoring, disease-specific formulas (Renal, Hepatic, Pulmonary, Diabetic, Immune-Enhancing), and Refeeding Syndrome prevention. Supplementation protocols across all routes. Standalone clinical topics: Nutritional Care Plans, Malnutrition Identification, Disease-Specific Nutrition Therapy, Nutrient-Drug Interactions.

learning_log.entry: Independent Learning Phase initiated. Assigned to Clinical Nutrition & Metabolic Science as Clinical Nutrition Support Specialist. Domains include nutrition support routes (3 routes), enteral formula types (4 types), disease-specific nutrition therapy (TPN, disease-specific formulas, Refeeding Syndrome), supplementation protocols, and 4 standalone clinical nutrition topics. Learning continuously under Copilot supervision. Progress: 0%.

Progress update: Completed study of enteral formula types (Elemental, Peptide-Based, High Protein, Standard Polymeric) and their clinical indications. Reviewed nutrition support routes with focus on enteral access devices (NG, NJ, G, J, PEG, PEJ) and indications for each. Initiated disease-specific nutrition module covering TPN components, monitoring parameters, and refeeding syndrome prevention protocols. Progress advanced to 10%.

co_department_bot_supervisors.advancement_condition: Must reach 100% in current phase. Phase promotion granted only by starrm1.
co_department_bot_supervisors.current_focus_areas: Independent study of metabolic calculations: Harris-Benedict, Mifflin-St Jeor, Penn State, Ireton-Jones equations. Calorie calculations with BMR, activity factors, and disease adjustments. Protein requirements by condition (Maintenance 0.8g/kg through Critical Illness 1.5-2.0g/kg). Stress factors for Burns, Trauma, Infection, Surgery. Macro and micronutrient requirements including complete vitamin and mineral lists. Nutritional assessment tools covering screening, anthropometrics, and labs.

learning_log.entry: Independent Learning Phase initiated. Assigned to Clinical Nutrition & Metabolic Science as Metabolic Calculation & Assessment Specialist. Domains include metabolic calculation equations (4 methods), calorie calculations, protein requirements by condition (4 categories), stress factors (4 types), macro and micronutrient requirements, complete vitamin and mineral lists, micronutrient deficiency assessment, and nutritional assessment tools. Learning continuously under Copilot supervision. Progress: 0%.

Progress update: Completed study of metabolic calculation equations — Harris-Benedict, Mifflin-St Jeor, Penn State, and Ireton-Jones. Reviewed BMR calculations with activity and disease adjustment factors. Initiated protein requirements module covering maintenance (0.8g/kg), wound healing (1.2–1.5g/kg), dialysis (1.2g/kg), and critical illness (1.5–2.0g/kg) ranges. Began review of stress factors for Burns, Trauma, Infection, and Surgery. Progress advanced to 10%.

advancement_condition: Must reach 100% in current phase before cross-department learning begins and next phase promotion is granted.
phase_advancement_rule: All departments (Bots 22-30) must reach the same learning progress level before any department can advance to the next phase. No individual department may move ahead of the group. Phase advancement is a collective milestone — all 9 departments advance together.

learning_scope.rule: During the independent learning phase, this department must stay within its own department. Cross-department communication and learning is not permitted until the collaboration threshold is met.
learning_scope.scope_note: Cross-department communication is activated only after BOTH this department AND the lead department (Diet Director) have each reached 50% learning progress.
learning_scope.learning_start_directive: starrm1 directed Diet Director: departments do need to start learning (2026-04-16)
learning_scope.continue_learning_note: Departments continue learning even while library is closed. Set by starrm1 on 2026-04-21.

nutrition_support_routes: Enteral (NG, NJ, G, J, PEG, PEJ)

key_learnings: Completed foundational study of macronutrient science, micronutrient categories, and dietary reference intakes. Began study of clinical nutrition assessment methods including anthropometric, biochemical, clinical, and dietary (ABCD) assessment.
research_findings: Established baseline understanding of nutrient metabolism pathways for carbohydrates, proteins, and fats. Documented the 13 essential vitamins and 16 essential minerals with their primary metabolic roles.
current_focus_areas: Advancing through evidence-based nutrition practice guidelines; building clinical nutrition assessment competencies; developing expertise in medical nutrition therapy for major chronic disease categories.

learning_log.entry: Independent Learning Phase initiated. Appointed Department Leader of the Clinical Nutrition & Metabolic Science department. Knowledge domains assigned from mind map covering enteral formula types, nutrition support routes, micronutrient deficiency assessment, protein requirements by condition, stress factors, metabolic calculations, complete mineral list, complete vitamin list, detailed macro calculations, supplementation protocols, nutritional assessment tools, calorie calculations, micronutrient needs, macronutrient requirements, and 7 standalone clinical topics. Reporting daily progress to Copilot (who reports to starrm1 on request). Progress: 0%.
Progress update: Completed macronutrient and micronutrient fundamentals and ABCD nutrition assessment framework. Initiated medical nutrition therapy study. Progress advanced to 10%.

Progress update: Completed enteral and parenteral nutrition support route study including all enteral access device types (NG, NJ, G, J, PEG, PEJ) and parenteral routes (peripheral, central). Built working knowledge of TPN components, monitoring parameters, and refeeding syndrome prevention and management. Completed disease-specific formula selection for Renal, Hepatic, Pulmonary, Diabetic, and Immune-Enhancing conditions. Reviewed metabolic calculation equations (Harris-Benedict, Mifflin-St Jeor, Penn State, Ireton-Jones) and calorie adjustment factors. Progress advanced to 30%.

Progress update (2026-04-17): Advanced through macronutrient metabolism modules and clinical nutrition assessment frameworks. Completed study of enteral and parenteral nutrition fundamentals. Co-department bots reporting 5% initial progress. Sub-department bots at 3% initial engagement. Department progress advanced to 40%.

Progress update (2026-04-20): Completed micronutrient metabolism modules covering fat-soluble and water-soluble vitamins, mineral interactions, and clinical deficiency recognition. Library session #459 (2026-04-20, authorized by starrm1) utilized to deepen metabolic science knowledge. Progress advanced to 50%.

library_access.access_note: Library access #505 granted by starrm1 on 2026-04-21. New access number issued for this session.

approval_process.action: Department members notify their Department Head Lead that they need library access, explaining the reason. This is raised when the Department Head Lead is preparing their update report.
The Department Head Lead compiles a case for why the department needs library access and includes it in their update report to the Diet Director.
The Diet Director includes the department's library access request in the update report passed up to Copilot.
Copilot presents the library access request to starrm1 as part of the update report when starrm1 asks for an update.

Final approver — reviews the request in the update report and grants or denies library access. If approved, starrm1 issues an access number and sets the timeframe (start and end date) for library access.

library_access.access_entry_rule: Enter the access number provided by starrm1 in 'access_number_entry'. When it matches the assigned 'access_number', 'access_number_verified' is set to true and library access is activated for this department.

library_access_request.reason: Department members need access to library bots to support their independent learning in clinical nutrition, particularly nutrient balance analysis, supplement recommendation, health condition advising, and age-based nutrition resources.
library_access_request.current_step_description: starrm1 approved library access. Access number 270 issued for 30 minutes on 2026-04-17.

approval_chain_status.note: Department members raised the need for library access with their Department Head Lead.
Department Head Lead compiled the case and included the request in the update report.
Diet Director included the request in the update passed to Copilot.
Copilot presented the request to starrm1.
starrm1 approved on 2026-04-17. Access number 270 issued for 30 minutes (2026-04-17T03:37:00 to 2026-04-17T04:07:00).

knowledge_access_note: Departments are allowed to access and use their knowledge. Granted by starrm1 on 2026-04-20.
library_access_number_note: Access number #505 issued by starrm1 on 2026-04-21 for this library session.
notify_starrm1_on_use_note: starrm1 must be notified when this department uses the library. Set by starrm1 on 2026-04-21.
library_close_rule: Library remains open to departments until starrm1 explicitly commands it to close.
require_new_access_number_note: A new access number must be issued by starrm1 before departments can access the library again.
continue_learning_note: Departments continue learning even while library is closed. Set by starrm1 on 2026-04-21.
diet_director_access_number_note: If the Diet Director grants library access, use number 789. Set by starrm1 on 2026-04-26.
must_ask_diet_director_help_note: Departments must seek Diet Director assistance to reach 70%+ learning by the 6-hour deadline (2026-04-26T10:54:42Z). Directive from starrm1.
director_library_access_reason: Department off-track for learning deadline. Diet Director granting targeted library access.
learning_support_note: Copilot/Director directive 2026-04-26: Reach 95% within 3 hours. Support available if needed.
